questions.stem: This labeled dissection, showing chambers and great vessels in two views, teaches the normal gross anatomy of which organ?

explanation.summary: The labeled superior/inferior vena cava, aorta, pulmonary artery and veins, atria, and ventricles identify this as a dissection of the heart, demonstrating the four-chamber cardiac anatomy.
explanation.whyCorrect: The labeled superior/inferior vena cava, aorta, pulmonary artery and veins, atria, and ventricles identify this as a dissection of the heart, demonstrating the four-chamber cardiac anatomy.
